features.name: Type Hint CLI Generation
features.description: Automatically generates CLI parameter definitions from Python function type hints and default values.

features.name: Rich Output Integration
features.description: Built-in Rich integration for styled terminal output, error messages, and help formatting.

features.name: Subcommand System
features.description: App-based subcommand registration with automatic discovery and nested command support.

features.name: Shell Autocompletion
features.description: Automatic shell completion script generation for Bash, Zsh, Fish, and PowerShell.

features.name: CLI Testing
features.description: Built-in test runner for invoking CLI commands programmatically and capturing output.

call_chain.role: Decorator that registers a function as a CLI command, storing it for later Click conversion.
Converts a Typer app into a Click command by introspecting registered functions' type hints.
Inspects function signature and builds TyperParameter objects from type annotations and defaults.
Factory for creating CLI options with help text, envvar, and validation from type metadata.
Factory for creating positional CLI arguments with type conversion and completion support.

type_hint_cli.explanation: Typer bridges Python type hints to Click parameters. When @app.command() is used, the decorated function is stored with its metadata. When the CLI is invoked, get_command() inspects each function's signature using get_params_from_function(). Python types are mapped to Click types: str→STRING, int→INT, Path→Path, bool→BOOL (as flags), Enum→Choice. Default values determine if a parameter is an Argument (required positional) or Option (--flag). The result is a fully-configured Click command that Typer delegates to for parsing and execution, while maintaining Typer's simpler API surface.

call_chain.role: Registers a sub-Typer app as a command group under the parent app.
Converts a Typer app with sub-apps into a Click Group with recursively resolved subcommands.
Handles leaf commands by converting single Typer functions into Click commands.
Entry point that builds the Click command tree and invokes it.

subcommands.explanation: Typer's subcommand system builds on Click's Group. When add_typer() is called, it stores a reference to the child Typer app. At invocation time, Typer.__call__() triggers get_group() which recursively converts all registered sub-apps into Click Groups and their commands into Click Commands. This produces a hierarchical Click command tree that handles parsing and dispatching. The result is that users get nested CLI subcommands (like 'app users create') with full help text, argument parsing, and completion — all derived from Python functions and type hints.
